requirements.text: WHEN operator supplies a numeric, zero-padded, mixed-case, or prefixed ticket reference, the system shall normalize it to an uppercase zero-padded ticket key before lookup.
WHEN operator supplies an explicit cross-project key or PROJECT/TICKET pair, the system shall resolve the requested ticket without requiring the current working directory to be inside that project.
WHEN cli.ticket.absolutePath is unset or false, the system shall display ticket paths relative to the project root.
WHEN cli.ticket.absolutePath is true, the system shall display ticket paths as absolute filesystem paths.
WHILE ~/.config/mdt/cli.toml is absent, the system shall default to relative-path display and color-enabled output for interactive terminals.
The system shall derive CLI status, priority, and type color mappings from the web UI badge definitions so category-to-color mapping remains aligned across interfaces.
WHEN mdt-cli create consumes user-provided title, slug, or standard input body text, the system shall persist that content as ticket data without executing, interpolating, or shell-expanding it.
WHILE output is redirected, piped, or --no-color is set, the system shall emit the same ticket information without ANSI color sequences.
IF operator supplies an invalid ticket key, THEN the system shall reject the command with a clear error that identifies the key as invalid and describes an accepted key format.
IF operator uses numeric shorthand outside a detected project and without explicit project context, THEN the system shall reject the request with an error that states project context is required.

WHEN shorthand ticket resolution depends on project auto-detection, the system shall search parent directories up to the filesystem root for project configuration before deciding that no project context exists.

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli project init in a folder that is not yet configured as a project, the system shall create project configuration for the current folder using provided code and name values or interactive defaults when omitted.

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli ticket get <key>, mdt-cli ticket <key>, mdt-cli t <key>, or mdt-cli <key> with a resolvable ticket reference, the system shall print the matching ticket title followed by labeled status, type, priority, phase, assignee, created date, modified date, and path fields.

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli project current or mdt-cli project inside a detected project, the system shall display the current project code, name, description, path, and relevant configuration values.

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli project get <code>, mdt-cli project info <code>, or mdt-cli project <code>, the system shall resolve the project code case-insensitively and display that project's information.

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli project with a single token after the namespace, the system shall reserve exact lowercase ls and list as project-list aliases and treat other tokens such as LS or LIST as project-code lookup inputs.

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli ticket create or mdt-cli create with a valid type, title, and optional priority or slug arguments, the system shall create one new ticket with default metadata and print the created ticket key and path.

WHEN operator passes type and priority tokens to mdt-cli ticket create or mdt-cli create, the system shall interpret the same create request regardless of the order in which those tokens are provided.
WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli ticket create or mdt-cli create with a slug but without a quoted title, the system shall derive the ticket title from the slug before creating the ticket.

WHEN mdt-cli ticket create or mdt-cli create receives piped standard input, the system shall generate ticket frontmatter and an H1 title, append the piped content as the ticket body, and skip template generation.

IF operator omits required command arguments or supplies an unrecognized create token, unsupported attribute-mutation form, unsupported attribute key, unsupported attribute operator, or unrecognized explicit project verb, THEN the system shall reject the command with a clear corrective message and a suggestion for the closest valid token when available.

The system shall accept only status, priority, phase, assignee, related, depends, blocks, impl-date, and impl-notes as mutable CLI attributes; only related, depends, and blocks may use += or -= operators, while all other mutable attributes use = only.

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli ticket attr <ticket> <attr-op><value>... or mdt-cli attr <ticket> <attr-op><value>... with supported attribute keys, the system shall normalize accepted value aliases, apply = to scalar attributes, apply =, +=, or -= to relation attributes, update all supplied attributes on the target ticket, and confirm the applied changes using pipe-separated format showing old and new values. When a value is unchanged, the system shall print an unchanged message and exit with code 0.

WHEN operator provides positional filter arguments after mdt-cli list or mdt-cli ticket list in key=value format, the system shall filter the ticket list using AND logic across different fields and comma-separated fuzzy matching within a single field. Filterable fields shall include status, priority, type, assignee, and epic (mapped to phaseEpic).

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli --guide or mdt-cli <namespace> --guide, the system shall print a generated command manual covering all registered commands, aliases, and usage examples for the requested scope. The guide shall be derived from the commander command tree and must not require manual editing.

WHEN output is written to an interactive terminal with color enabled, the system shall render ticket titles in white bold, ticket keys in light-cyan bold, project codes in dark cyan bold, project identifiers in gray, project titles in white bold, project descriptions in normal color, and file paths in gray. Status, priority, and type indicators shall use color categories that match the web UI badge mappings. Long status values shall use abbreviated display labels in list views (e.g., Partially Implemented → Part. Impl).

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli ticket list, mdt-cli list, mdt-cli ls, or mdt-cli ticket ls inside a detected project, the system shall display up to 10 tickets sorted newest-first by modified date in compact form and report the project ticket count. Each ticket shall show the key and title on one line, followed by an indented pipe-separated metadata line with fixed-width columns (status 11, type 13, priority 8) and optional phase. The system shall support --all to show every ticket, --limit N to override the default count, --files to show file paths only, and --info to show ticket info without file paths. Positional filter arguments in key=value format (e.g., status=implemented priority=high) shall filter tickets using AND across fields and comma-separated fuzzy matching within a field.

WHEN operator invokes mdt-cli project ls or mdt-cli project list, the system shall display each discovered project with its code in described color, title in white bold, description in normal color, and filesystem path in gray. The project identifier shall not appear in list output. Project detail views (project current, project get, project info) shall display the full identifier in gray.

scenarios.title: Initialize a project in the current folder
scenarios.given: Given I am in a folder that does not yet contain project configuration
scenarios.when: When I run mdt-cli project init MDT Markdown Ticket Board
scenarios.then: Then the current folder should receive project configuration for code MDT and name Markdown Ticket Board

scenarios.title: Render absolute ticket paths when configured
scenarios.given: Given cli.ticket.absolutePath is set to true
scenarios.then: Then I should see the ticket path as an absolute filesystem path

scenarios.title: View a ticket from detected project via bare shortcut
scenarios.given: Given I am in a project directory that contains ticket MDT-012
scenarios.then: Then I should see MDT-012 with its title and labeled status, type, priority, phase, assignee, created date, modified date, and relative path

scenarios.title: View a ticket across projects via canonical ticket get command
scenarios.given: Given I am outside the target project and ticket API-012 exists
scenarios.when: When I run mdt-cli ticket get API-12
scenarios.then: Then I should see API-012 with its title and labeled fields without requiring current-directory project context

scenarios.title: Show current project details via project shortcut
scenarios.given: Given I am in a configured project directory
scenarios.then: Then I should see the current project code, name, description, path, and relevant configuration values

scenarios.title: Resolve project lookup shortcut without colliding with list aliases
scenarios.given: Given a project with code LS is registered
scenarios.when: When I run mdt-cli project LS
scenarios.then: Then I should see the LS project information instead of the project list

scenarios.title: Create a ticket via canonical ticket create with order-independent tokens
scenarios.given: Given I am in a project directory that can create new tickets
scenarios.when: When I run mdt-cli ticket create feature/high add-dark-mode
scenarios.then: Then a new ticket should be created with the derived title Add dark mode, the requested priority, and a reported key and path

scenarios.title: Create a ticket from piped standard input via the create alias
scenarios.given: Given I am in a project directory and I pipe markdown content into the CLI
scenarios.when: When I run cat spec.md | mdt-cli create feature 'CLI tool'
scenarios.then: Then a new ticket should be created with generated frontmatter and title, followed by the literal piped body content and a reported key and path

scenarios.title: List tickets via canonical ticket list command
scenarios.given: operator is inside a detected project and invokes mdt-cli ticket list
scenarios.when: the command executes with no filters, with positional filters such as status=implemented priority=high, or with --files, --info, --all, or --limit flags
scenarios.then: the system displays up to 10 tickets sorted newest-first matching all applied filters, or all tickets with --all, or N tickets with --limit N, and reports the project ticket count

scenarios.title: Update ticket attributes with set, add, and remove operators through canonical ticket attr and the attr alias
scenarios.given: operator invokes mdt-cli ticket attr <ticket> <attr-op><value>... or mdt-cli attr <ticket> <attr-op><value>...
scenarios.when: the system applies all attribute mutations to the target ticket
scenarios.then: the system prints a pipe-separated confirmation showing each changed field with old and new values, or prints an unchanged message and exits code 0 when no value changed

scenarios.title: List discovered projects via project list aliases
scenarios.given: operator invokes mdt-cli project ls or mdt-cli project list
scenarios.when: the system resolves all discovered projects
scenarios.then: each project is displayed with its code in described color, identifier in gray parentheses, title in white, description in normal color, and filesystem path in gray

scenarios.title: Render colored ticket output with relative paths
scenarios.given: operator views a ticket or ticket list on an interactive terminal with color enabled
scenarios.then: ticket titles appear in white, ticket keys in light-blue, project codes in dark cyan, and file paths in gray, with relative paths by default

scenarios.title: Filter ticket list by multiple criteria with fuzzy matching
scenarios.given: operator invokes mdt-cli list with positional filters such as status=impl priority=high type=architecture
scenarios.when: the system matches tickets against all filter fields using AND across fields and fuzzy matching within each field
scenarios.then: only tickets satisfying all filter conditions are displayed, with status=impl matching implemented and status=implemented,in_progress matching tickets with either status

scenarios.title: Show generated command guide via --guide flag
scenarios.given: operator invokes mdt-cli --guide or mdt-cli ticket --guide
scenarios.when: the system collects all registered commands from the commander tree
scenarios.then: a formatted command manual is printed covering all commands, aliases, and usage examples for the requested scope without requiring manual editing

obligations.title: CLI output formatting shall centralize labeled field rendering, relative-vs-absolute path policy, and TTY color gating while keeping status, priority, and type color categories aligned with the web badge stylesheet.
Register the CLI as a workspace package with a dedicated bin entry, bootstrap a commander program at the entrypoint, and normalize supported shortcut argv before framework parse.

Executable acceptance for the CLI shall run through repo-native CLI E2E tests in the CLI package using @mdt/shared/test-lib, cover both canonical commander commands and supported shortcut forms, and avoid introducing bats or browser-only Playwright as the primary harness.
CLI shorthand ticket and project resolution shall consume the shared root-up project detection rule through ProjectService rather than a private detector copy.

Ticket view and list commands shall use a commander ticket subtree plus a small shortcut normalizer for bare-key and alias forms, resolve project context through ProjectService, and read ticket data through TicketService instead of duplicating file traversal logic.

Ticket create and attr shall be registered under the commander ticket subtree with top-level create and attr aliases, while attr parsing translates =, +=, and -= tokens into the shared AttrOperation model, routes writes through TicketService, and isolates STDIN body capture from command dispatch.

The project command shall be implemented as a commander subtree that owns current-project info, project listing, explicit get or info verbs, bare project-code shortcuts, and current-folder initialization while routing project reads through ProjectService and init through ProjectManager.
The CLI shall expose a --guide flag at global and per-namespace scope that generates a command manual from the registered commander tree, including all commands, aliases, and usage examples.

The ticket list command shall support default truncation to 10 tickets newest-first, positional filter arguments with AND cross-field and comma-separated fuzzy within-field matching on status, priority, type, assignee, and epic, --all and --limit flags for paging control, and --files and --info output modes.

TicketService.listTickets shall accept expanded TicketFilters (assignee, phaseEpic), fuzzy matching semantics, sort by dateModified newest-first, and limit/offset pagination. CLI must only parse positional key=value args into TicketFilters and pass them to the shared method; filtering, sorting, and pagination logic must remain in shared.

testPlans.title: Ticket read and list CLI flows
Ticket list filtering, paging, and output modes
Attr command output format and no-op behavior
CLI color scheme per element type

tasks.title: Bootstrap CLI package and commander tree
Implement ticket create and stdin flow
Implement ticket attr parser and mutation flow
Add CLI E2E harness and suites
Implement project namespace on shared project services
Add --guide flag with generated command manual
Refine attr output format and element-level color scheme
Add E2E suites for list enhancements, guide, attr output, and color scheme
Implement formatter, color policy, and CLI config
Implement ticket read and list commands on shared services
Enhance ticket list with shared filters, fuzzy matching, sort, and pagination
